key_insight: The paper shows that finite-element discretization can be reformulated as a tensor-network variational problem, turning many analytic operator equations into linear matrix equations with a shared algebraic structure.
engineering_relevance: This creates a general surrogate framework for solving differential, nonlinear, memory, and delay equations while preserving key FE properties like continuity and Neumann boundary conditions.
method: Discretize analytic operator equations with finite elements to generate multilinear interaction tensors.
Recast the resulting system as a weighted-residual optimization over tensor-network degrees of freedom.
Use matrix-product-state solvers to approximate 1D linear and nonlinear diffusion problems.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that matrix-product-state calculations for one-dimensional linear and nonlinear diffusion reproduce conventional solutions with controlled error.
watch_item: The demonstrated results are for 1D diffusion examples, so broader accuracy, scalability, and implementation cost in higher-dimensional or strongly nonlinear problems still need validation.
paper_title: Tensor-Network Finite Elements for Analytic Operator Equations
